---
title: "ai-minerals"
subtitle: "Mineral prospectivity mapping on public open data"
---

A portfolio artifact demonstrating competent engagement with the problem space that [KoBold Metals](https://koboldmetals.com/), [ExploreTech](https://exploretech.ai/), and [Earth AI](https://earth-ai.com/) work in. Public open data, Random Forest + SHAP, spatial block CV, external blind test — and an honest decomposition of where the standard approach fails.

## Two regions

- **v1 — [Eastern Alaska porphyry Cu-Mo-Au-Ag](notebooks/eastak/eastak_porphyry_prospectivity.qmd)** — Tanacross / Mt. Hayes / Nabesna quadrangles. The story is the external blind test that reveals an exploration-bias trap the training-label metrics hide.
- **v2 — [BC Golden Triangle (Cu-Au porphyry + epithermal + skarn + VMS)](notebooks/bcgt/bcgt_porphyry_prospectivity.qmd)** — Brucejack / KSM / Red Chris / Galore Creek / Eskay Creek. Multi-deposit-class model validated against a **distribution** of 241 post-2015 drill holes (154 intersected positives + 87 drilled-negative), not the v1 N=1 anecdote.

## v1 headline finding (for context)

Under standard metrics the Random Forest looks good: ROC-AUC 0.88 ± 0.2 on 20-km spatial-block CV, top-2 % of area captures 80 % of known positives, SHAP attributes correctly to the textbook porphyry pathfinder suite.

The external blind test — Kenorland `23ETD062`, a 2023 porphyry drill intersection that postdates every training label — scores **P=0.012, 62nd percentile**. The feature diagnostic traces this to **exploration bias in three channels** (count / NaN-vs-value / magnitude-within-explored), with the third being dominant. A v1.1 sensitivity battery tests four remediations (drop counts, `has_data` indicators, no-geochem baseline, PU bagging); none rescues the blind test, though PU moves Kenorland 8 percentile points higher.

The honest posture: a pseudo-supervised MPM pipeline at 500 m regional resolution can't distinguish a distal low-grade porphyry hit from background regardless of feature engineering. The v1.2 direction (prospect-scale alteration mineralogy via ASTER/EMIT SWIR, physics-consistent geophysical inversions via SimPEG, POMDP-style sequential drill planning) is the direction KoBold and ExploreTech actually build toward.

## Secondary material

- [Design proposal](design/mvp-proposal.md) — scoping document, v1 build plan, v1.1 findings trace, v2 plan.
- Eastern Alaska build log — [Data exploration](notebooks/eastak/data_exploration.qmd) → [Baseline model](notebooks/eastak/baseline_model.qmd) → [Random Forest + SHAP](notebooks/eastak/random_forest_and_shap.qmd) → [Validation + sensitivities](notebooks/eastak/validation.qmd). Integrated report rolls all four together.
- BC Golden Triangle — [Data exploration](notebooks/bcgt/data_exploration.qmd) → [Integrated report](notebooks/bcgt/bcgt_porphyry_prospectivity.qmd).
- [Research briefs](research/) — independent writeups on the three target companies, the competitive landscape, MPM state of the art, and the open-data stack.

## Reproduce

```bash
git clone <this-repo>
cd ai-minerals
uv sync
uv run python -m ai_minerals.data.fetch.all      # ~5 GB, ~1 hr
uv run python -m ai_minerals.features.assemble   # rebuilds the parquet
QUARTO_PYTHON=.venv/bin/python quarto render     # renders this site
```

See [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) for project rules. License: MIT.
